message:
fuzz: add fuzz_planarity_coloring target with runtime invariants

New structure-aware fuzz target exercising:

- fnx_algorithms::is_planar (LR-planarity test)
- fnx_algorithms::greedy_color and greedy_color_with_strategy

Beyond the no-panic invariant, asserts:

Coloring family
  - Result is a *proper* coloring: for every edge (u,v), color[u]
    != color[v].
  - Result is *total*: every node receives a color.
  - Bound: num_colors == 1 + max_color (or 0 for empty graph).
  - All five strategies (largest_first, smallest_last,
    random_sequential, DSATUR, lexicographic) produce proper
    colorings on the same input.
  - Determinism: the same strategy run twice on the same graph
    yields identical colorings.

Planarity family
  - is_planar is deterministic across calls.
  - n <= 4 ⇒ always planar (K_4 is planar).
  - For n >= 3, |E| > 3|V|-6 ⇒ not planar (Euler bound).
  - Edge-deletion monotonicity: a planar graph stays planar after
    removing an edge. The fuzzer drops a randomly chosen edge from
    a planar input and re-checks planarity.

Includes a coverage-minimized seed corpus of 435 inputs harvested
from a 60s fuzzing pass (libfuzzer cmin), spanning sparse and dense
topologies, near-bound-saturated graphs around |E| ≈ 3n-6, and the
empty-graph degenerate case.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
